{"id":"project-LICHEN-3h1o","title":"Decoder accepts extra trailing bytes without warning","description":"File: python/src/lichen/gateway/compact_cot.py
Lines: 246-247, 296-299

The decoder checks for minimum length but accepts extra trailing bytes:

PLI decoder (line 246-247):
```python
if len(data) < PLI_TOTAL_SIZE:
    raise DecodeError(f'PLI too short: {len(data)} < {PLI_TOTAL_SIZE}')
# Accepts len(data) > 17 without warning
```

Chat decoder (line 296-299):
```python
if len(data) < total_size:
    raise DecodeError(f'Chat message truncated: {len(data)} < {total_size}')
# Accepts len(data) > total_size without warning
```

Impact:
1. Corrupted messages with garbage suffix accepted silently
2. Protocol version mismatches go undetected (new format has extra fields)
3. Buffer reuse bugs in callers not caught

Recommendation: Either:
1. Warn on trailing bytes (preferred for debugging):
```python
if len(data) > PLI_TOTAL_SIZE:
    warnings.warn(f'PLI has {len(data) - PLI_TOTAL_SIZE} trailing bytes')
```

2. Strict reject:
```python
if len(data) != PLI_TOTAL_SIZE:
    raise DecodeError(f'PLI wrong size: {len(data)} != {PLI_TOTAL_SIZE}')
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:17:59.67298Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:17:59.673008Z"}
